recommendation: Use current canonicalToolId and target language
example_or_detail: Resolve from page context, not from visible slug text.
/tools/name-generator/ selecting fr goes to /fr/tools/generateur-de-noms/.
If fr localized page missing, send to /tools/name-generator/ rather than /fr/.
If same-tool route is unavailable or intentionally withheld, use /fr/tools/generateurs-de-noms/ or equivalent hub.

recommendation: Language homepage only as last resort
example_or_detail: Use /fr/ only when no safe same-tool or same-hub destination exists.
If English tool is noindex and not localized, language switch may stay on English noindex same tool or use a safe hub; never create a thin localized indexable page.
A removed/merged source should switch to localized version of its redirect target if available.
Alternates include only language versions of the exact same tool that are ready and indexable/noindex-consistent.
Alternates include same topical hub across languages, not loosely similar categories.
For tool pages x-default points to /tools/{english-slug}/.
/fr/tools/generateur-de-noms/ canonicalizes to https://taptogen.com/fr/tools/generateur-de-noms/.
draft, keyword-review, native-review, ready, hold, noindex-only, retired. Generate public pages only for ready or approved noindex-only.
Every hreflang alternate must link back to the current page in its own alternate set.
Every language-switch URL must resolve to a generated page or approved redirect.
Audit that tool pages do not switch to language homepages unless fallbackReason is recorded.
Each language has unique localized tool slugs; hubs and tools cannot collide within the same path namespace.
